author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VII. 25–36.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You remember, beloved, in the former discourses,—for it was both read in the Gospel and also discussed by us according to our ability,—how that the Lord Jesus went up to the feast-day, as it were in secret, not because He feared lest He should be laid hold of,—He who had the power not to be laid hold of,—but to signify that even in that very feast which was celebrated by the Jews He Himself was hidden, and that the mystery of the feast was His own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the passage read to-day then, that which was supposed to be timidity appeared as power; for He spoke openly on the feast-day, so that the crowds marvelled, and said that which we have heard when the passage was read: “Is not this he whom they sought to kill?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, lo, he speaketh openly, and they say nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do the rulers know indeed that this is the Christ?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who knew with what fierceness He was sought after, wondered by what power He was kept from being taken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, not fully understanding His power, they fancied it was the knowledge of the rulers, that these rulers knew Him to be the very Christ, and that for this reason they spared Him whom they had with so much eagerness sought out to be put to death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then those same persons who had said, “Did the rulers know that this is the Christ?” proposed a question among themselves, by which it appeared to them that He was not the Christ; for they said in addition, “But we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As to how this opinion among the Jews arose, that “when Christ comes, no man knoweth whence He is” (for it did not arise without reason), if we consider the Scriptures, we find, brethren, that the Holy Scriptures have declared of Christ that “He shall be called a Nazarene.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore they foretold whence He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, if we seek the place of His nativity, as that whence He is by birth, neither was this hidden from the Jews, because of the Scriptures which had foretold these things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the Magi, on the appearing of a star, sought Him out to worship Him, they came to Herod and told him what they sought and what they meant: and he, having called together those who had knowledge of the law, inquired of them where Christ should be born: they told him, “In Bethlehem of Judah,” and also brought forward the prophetic testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, therefore, the prophets had foretold both the place where the origin of His flesh was, and the place where His mother would bring Him forth, whence did spring that opinion among the Jews which we have just heard, but from this, that the Scriptures had proclaimed beforehand, and had foretold both?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In respect of His being man, the Scriptures foretold whence He should be; in respect of His being God, this was hidden from the ungodly, and it required godly men to discover it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, they said this, “When Christ comes, no man knoweth whence He is,” because that which was spoken by Isaiah produced this opinion in them, viz. “And His generation, who shall tell?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, the Lord Himself made answer to both, that they both did, and also did not know whence He was; that He might testify to the holy prophecy which before was predicted of Him, both as to the humanity of infirmity and also as to the divinity of majesty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear, therefore, the word of the Lord, brethren; see how He confirmed to them both what they said, “We know this man whence he is,” and also what they said, “When Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then cried Christ in the temple, saying, Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but He that sent me is true, whom ye know not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is to say, ye both know me, and ye know me not; ye both know whence I am, and ye know not whence I am.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ye know whence I am: Jesus of Nazareth, whose parents also ye knew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in this case, the birth of the Virgin alone was hidden, to whom, however, her husband was witness; for the same was able faithfully to declare this, who was also able as a husband to be jealous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, this birth of the Virgin excepted, they knew all that in Jesus pertains to man: His face was known, His country was known, His family was known; where He was born was to be known by inquiry.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rightly then did He say, “Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am,” according to the flesh and form of man which He bore; but according to His divinity, “And I am not come of myself, but He that sent me is true, whom ye know not;” but yet that ye may know Him, believe on Him whom He has sent, and ye will know Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, “No man has seen God at any time, except the only-begotten Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, He hath declared Him:” and, “None knoweth the Father but the Son, and he to whom the Son wills to reveal Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lastly, when He had said, “But He that sent me is true, whom ye know not,” in order to show them whence they might know that which they did not know, He subjoined, “I know Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore seek from me to know Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why is it that I know Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Because I am from Him, and He sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Gloriously has He shown both.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I am from Him,” said He; because the Son is from the Father, and whatever the Son is, He is of Him whose Son He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence we say that the Lord Jesus is God of God: we do not say that the Father is God of God, but simply God: and we say that the Lord Jesus is Light of Light; we do not say that the Father is Light of Light, but simply Light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, to this belongs that which He said “I am from Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But as to my being seen of you in the flesh, “He sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When thou hearest “He sent me,” do not understand a difference of nature to be meant, but the authority of Him that begets.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then they sought to take Him: but no man laid hands on Him, because His hour was not yet come;” that is, because He was not willing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what is this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “His hour was not yet come”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord was not born under fate.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is not to be believed concerning thee, much less concerning Him by whom thou wast made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thy hour is His good will, what is His hour but His good will?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He meant not therefore an hour in which He should be forced to die, but that in which He would deign to be put to death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But He was awaiting the time in which He should die, for He awaited also the time in which He should be born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle, speaking of this time, says, “But when the fullness of time came, God sent His Son.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this cause many say, Why did not Christ come before?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom we must make answer, Because the fullness of time had not yet come, while He by whom the times were made sets their bounds; for He knew when He ought to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the first place, it was necessary that He should be foretold through a long series of times and years; for it was not something insignificant that was to come: He who was to be ever held, had to be for a long time foretold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The greater the judge that was coming, the longer the train of heralds that preceded him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, when the fullness of time came, He also came who was to deliver us from time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For being delivered from time, we shall come to that eternity where there is no time: there it is not said, When shall the hour come, for the day is everlasting, a day which is neither preceded by a yesterday, nor cut off by a morrow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in this world days roll on, some are passing away, others come; none abides; and the moments in which we are speaking drive out one another in turn, nor stands the first syllable for the second to sound.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since we began to speak we are somewhat older, and without doubt I am just now older than I was in the morning; thus, nothing stands, nothing remains fixed in time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore ought we to love Him by whom the times were made, that we may be delivered from time and be fixed in eternity, where there is no more changeableness of times.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Great, therefore, is the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ, in that for our sakes He was made in time, by whom the times were made; that He was made among all things, by whom all things were made; that He became what He made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was made what He had made; for He was made man who had made man, lest what He had made should perish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: According to this dispensation, the hour of His birth had now come, and He was born; but not yet had come the hour of His suffering, therefore not yet had He suffered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In short, that ye may know that the words refer, not to the necessity of His dying, but to His power,—I speak this for the sake of some who, when they hear “His hour was not yet come,” are determined on believing in fate, and their hearts become infatuated;—that ye may know, then, that it was His power of dying, recollect the passion, look at Him crucified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: While hanging on the tree, He said, “I thirst.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They, having heard this, offered to Him on the cross vinegar by a sponge on a reed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He received it, and said, “It is finished;” and, bowing His head, gave up the ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see His power of dying, that He waited for this—until all things should be fulfilled that had been foretold concerning Him—to take place before His death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the prophet had said, “They gave me gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He waited for all these things to be fulfilled: after they were completed, He said, “It is finished;” and He departed by power, because He came not by necessity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence some wondered more at this His power to die than at His ability to work miracles.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they came to the cross to take the bodies down from the tree, for the Sabbath was drawing near, and the thieves were found still living.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The punishment of the cross was so much the harder because it tortured men so long, and all that were crucified were killed by a lingering death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the thieves, that they might not remain on the tree, were forced to die by having their legs broken, that they might be taken down thence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, however, was found to be already dead, and the men marvelled; and they who despised Him when living, so wondered at Him when dead, that some of them said, “Truly this was the Son of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence also that, brethren, where He says to those that seek Him, “I am He;” and they, going backward, all fell to the ground?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently there was in Him supreme power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor was He forced to die at an hour; but He waited the hour on which His will might fittingly be done, not that on which necessity might be fulfilled against His will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But many of the people believed on Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord made whole the humble and the poor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The rulers were mad, and therefore they not only did not acknowledge the Physician, but even were eager to slay Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There was a certain crowd of people which quickly saw its own sickness, and without delay recognized His remedy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See what that very crowd, moved by His miracles, said: “When Christ cometh will He do more signs than these?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely, unless there will be two Christs, this is the Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently, in saying these things, they believed on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those rulers, having heard the assurance of the multitude, and that murmuring noise of the people in which Christ was being glorified, “sent officers to take Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To take whom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Him not yet willing to be taken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because then they could not take Him while He would not, they were sent to hear Him teaching.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Teaching what?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then said Jesus, Yet a little while I am with you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What ye wish to do now ye will do, but not just now; because I am not just now willing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why am I now as yet unwilling?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because “yet a little while I am with you; and then I go unto Him that sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I must complete my dispensation, and in this manner come to my suffering.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here He has already foretold His resurrection; for they would not acknowledge Him when present, and afterwards they sought Him when they saw the multitude already believing on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For great signs were wrought, even when the Lord was risen again and ascended into heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then mighty deeds were done by His disciples, but He wrought by them as He wrought by Himself: since, indeed, He had said to them, “Without me ye can do nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When that lame man who sat at the gate rose up at Peter’s voice, and walked on his feet, so that men marvelled, Peter spoke to them to this effect, that it was not by his own power that he did this, but in the virtue of Him whom they slew.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many pricked in the heart said, “What shall we do?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they saw themselves bound by an immense crime of impiety, since they slew Him whom they ought to have revered and worshipped; and this crime they thought inexpiable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A great wickedness indeed it was, the thought of which might make them despair; yet it did not behove them to despair, for whom the Lord, as He hung on the cross, deigned to pray.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He had said, “Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saw some who were His own among many who were aliens; for these He sought pardon, from whom at the time He was still receiving injury.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He regarded not that He was being put to death by them, but only that He was dying for them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was a great thing that was forgiven them, it was a great thing that was done by them and for them, so that no man should despair of the forgiveness of his sin when they who slew Christ obtained pardon.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ died for us, but surely He was not put to death by us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But those men indeed saw Christ dying by their own villany; and yet they believed on Christ pardoning their villanies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Until they drank the blood they had shed, they despaired of their own salvation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore said He this: “Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, ye cannot come;” because they were to seek Him after the resurrection, being pricked in their heart with remorse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor did He say “where I will be,” but “where I am.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ was always in that place whither He was about to return; for He came in such manner that He did not depart from that place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence He says in another place, “No man has ascended into heaven, but He who came down from heaven, the Son of man who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not, who was in heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He spoke on the earth, and declared that He was at the same time in heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He came in such wise that He departed not thence; and He so returned as not to abandon us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What do ye marvel at?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is God’s doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For man, as regards his body, is in a place, and departs from a place; and when he comes to another place, he will not be in that place whence he came: but God fills all things, and is all everywhere; He is not held in places according to space.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless the Lord Christ was, as regards His visible flesh, on the earth: as regards His invisible majesty, He was in heaven and on earth; and therefore He says, “Where I am, thither ye cannot come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor did He say, “Ye shall not be able.” but “ye are not able to come;” for at that time they were such as were not able.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that ye may know that this was not said to cause despair, He said something of the same kind also to His disciples: “Whither I go ye cannot come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet while praying in their behalf, He said, “Father, I will that where I am they also may be with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And, finally, this He expounded to Peter, and says to him, “Whither I go thou canst not follow me now, but thou shalt follow me hereafter.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then said the Jews,” not to Him, but “to themselves, Whither will this man go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersion among the Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they knew not what they said; but, it being His will, they prophesied.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord was indeed about to go to the Gentiles, not by His bodily presence, but still with His feet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What were His feet?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those which Saul desired to trample upon by persecution, when the Head cried out to him, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this saying that He said, “Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the Lord said this they knew not, and yet they did predict something that was to be without knowing it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is what the Lord said that they knew not the place, if place however it must be called, which is the bosom of the Father, from which Christ never departed; nor were they competent to conceive where Christ was, whence Christ never withdrew, whither He was to return, where He was all the while dwelling.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How was it possible for the human heart to conceive this, least of all to explain it with the tongue?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, they in no wise understood; and yet by occasion of this they foretold our salvation, that the Lord would go to the dispersion of the Gentiles, and would fulfill that which they read but did not understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “A people whom I have not known served me, and by the hearing of the ear obeyed me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They before whose eyes He was, heard Him not; those heard Him in whose ears He was sounded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of that Church of the Gentiles which was to come, the woman that had the issue of blood was a type: she touched and was not seen; she was not known and yet was healed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was in reality a figure what the Lord asked: “Who touched me?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if not knowing, He healed her as unknown: so has He done also to the Gentiles.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We did not get to know Him in the flesh, yet we have been made worthy to eat His flesh, and to be members in His flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what way?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because He sent to us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His heralds, His disciples, His servants, His redeemed whom He created, but whom He redeemed, His brethren also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have said but little of all that they are: His own members, Himself; for He sent to us His own members, and He made us His members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, Christ has not been among us with the bodily form which the Jews saw and despised; because this also was said concerning Him, even as the apostle says: “Now I say that Christ was a minister of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises made unto the fathers.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He owed it to have come to those by whose fathers and to whose fathers He was promised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this reason He says also Himself: “I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what says the apostle in the following words?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And that the Gentiles might glorify God for His mercy.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, moreover, saith the Lord Himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Other sheep I have which are not of this fold.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who had said, “I am not sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of Israel,” how has He other sheep to which He was not sent, except that He intimated that He was not sent to show His bodily presence but to the Jews only, who saw and killed Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet many of them, both before and afterwards, believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The first harvest was winnowed from the cross, that there might be a seed whence another harvest might spring up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But at this present time, when roused by the fame of the gospel, and by its goodly odor, His faithful ones among all nations believe, He shall be the expectation of the Gentiles, when He shall come who has already come; when He shall be seen by all, He who was then not seen by some, by some was seen; when He shall come to judge who came to be judged; when He shall come to distinguish who came not to be distinguish ed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ was not discerned by the ungodly, but was condemned with the ungodly; for it was said concerning Him, “He was accounted among the wicked.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The robber escaped, Christ was condemned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who was loaded with criminal accusations received pardon; He who has released from their crimes all who confess Him, was condemned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless even the cross itself, if thou considerest it well, was a judgment-seat; for the Judge being set up in the middle, one thief who believed was delivered, the other who reviled was condemned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already He signified what He is to do with the quick and the dead: some He will set on His right hand and others on His left.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That thief was like those that shall be on the left hand, the other like those that shall be on the right.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was undergoing judgment, and He threatened judgment.
